questions.question: Which 2.4 GHz channels are non-overlapping in common planning?
questions.explanation: Channels 1, 6, and 11 are the standard non-overlapping set in 2.4 GHz.

correct.explanation: Channels 1, 6, and 11 are the standard non-overlapping set in 2.4 GHz.

incorrect.explanation: 1, 2, 3 uses the wrong value. The tested fact calls for 6 — not 1.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "1, 2, 3" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: 6, 7, 8 uses the wrong value. The tested fact calls for 1 — not 6.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "6, 7, 8" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: 11, 12, 13 only in all regions uses the wrong value. The tested fact calls for 1 — not 11.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "11, 12, 13 only in all regions" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Using adjacent 2.4 GHz channels (e.g. 3 and 4) — They overlap — stick to 1, 6, 11 for non-overlapping 20 MHz cells

questions.question: 5 GHz Wi‑Fi typically offers…
choices: Longer range than 2.4 with fewer channels
More channels and higher throughput but shorter range
questions.explanation: 5 GHz has more spectrum and speed; 2.4 GHz penetrates farther.

correct.explanation: 5 GHz has more spectrum and speed; 2.4 GHz penetrates farther.

incorrect.explanation: Longer range than 2.4 with fewer channels reflects a common trap: Using adjacent 2.4 GHz channels (e.g. 3 and 4). They overlap — stick to 1, 6, 11 for non-overlapping 20 MHz cells.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Using adjacent 2.4 GHz channels (e.g. 3 and 4)

incorrect.explanation: No encryption support misses what this question tests. 5 GHz has more spectrum and speed; 2.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "No encryption support" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Only WEP misses what this question tests. 5 GHz has more spectrum and speed; 2.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Only WEP" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Using adjacent 2.4 GHz channels (e.g. 3 and 4) — They overlap — stick to 1, 6, 11 for non-overlapping 20 MHz cells

questions.question: WPA3 improves on WPA2 primarily with…
questions.explanation: WPA3 uses SAE for more robust authentication vs WPA2-PSK.

correct.explanation: WPA3 uses SAE for more robust authentication vs WPA2-PSK.

incorrect.explanation: WEP is deprecated and insecure — CCNA expects WPA2/WPA3 for real deployments.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Choosing WEP for compatibility

incorrect.explanation: Open unsecured networks misses what this question tests. WPA3 uses SAE for more robust authentication vs WPA2-PSK.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Open unsecured networks" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Removing AES misses what this question tests. WPA3 uses SAE for more robust authentication vs WPA2-PSK.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Removing AES" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Choosing WEP for compatibility — WEP is insecure; use WPA2 minimum, WPA3 preferred

questions.question: True or False: WEP is acceptable for modern enterprise WLANs.

incorrect.explanation: This statement is True, but the tested fact is: False — WEP is cryptographically broken.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "True" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Choosing WEP for compatibility — WEP is insecure; use WPA2 minimum, WPA3 preferred

incorrect.explanation: Wi‑Fi 3 uses the wrong value. The tested fact calls for 802 — not 3.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Wi‑Fi 3" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Wi‑Fi 4 uses the wrong value. The tested fact calls for 802 — not 4.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Wi‑Fi 4" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Wi‑Fi 5 uses the wrong value. The tested fact calls for 802 — not 5.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Wi‑Fi 5" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: Match band, range, and standard to the scenario — WEP is never the right modern answer.

questions.question: The SSID is…
questions.explanation: SSID identifies the WLAN broadcast by the AP.

correct.explanation: SSID identifies the WLAN broadcast by the AP.

incorrect.explanation: The encryption key misses what this question tests. SSID identifies the WLAN broadcast by the AP.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "The encryption key" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: The MAC of the AP misses what this question tests. SSID identifies the WLAN broadcast by the AP.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "The MAC of the AP" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: The VLAN ID only misses what this question tests. SSID identifies the WLAN broadcast by the AP.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "The VLAN ID only" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: This stem tests ssid — exam distractors swap similar terms; anchor on: SSID identifies the WLAN broadcast by the AP.

questions.question: Doubling channel width from 20 MHz to 40 MHz generally…
choices: Increases potential throughput but uses more spectrum
questions.explanation: Wider channels carry more data but reduce the number of non-overlapping channels.

correct.explanation: Wider channels carry more data but reduce the number of non-overlapping channels.

incorrect.explanation: Reduces throughput misses what this question tests. Wider channels carry more data but reduce the number of non-overlapping channels.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Reduces throughput" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Eliminates interference misses what this question tests. Wider channels carry more data but reduce the number of non-overlapping channels.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Eliminates interference" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Disables 5 GHz misses what this question tests. Wider channels carry more data but reduce the number of non-overlapping channels.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Disables 5 GHz" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: More AP power always fixes coverage — Higher power increases interference and sticky clients; proper placement and channel plan matter
